meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no marketing skills (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no marketing skills. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit for Students: Master Marketing & Coding
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no marketing' barrier. Learn to build a SaaS starter kit using Antigravity and Claude Code. Expert guide for intermediate student developers.
content.answer_block: Stop letting a lack of marketing skills paralyze your development. By building a high-utility SaaS starter kit, you create a product that solves a real developer pain point. This approach shifts your focus from 'selling to strangers' to 'building for peers,' effectively turning your technical portfolio into your primary marketing engine.
content.intro:
You’ve spent countless hours mastering syntax, debugging complex state management, and agonizing over database schemas. Yet, when you look at your GitHub, the silence is deafening. The invisible wall isn't your code—it’s the marketing chasm. You feel like a ghost in the machine, building perfect products that nobody sees.

The relief lies in changing your perspective: stop thinking like a marketer and start thinking like a product engineer. By building a SaaS starter kit, you solve the 'no marketing' problem by creating a tool that other students and junior devs desperately need. This is how you bridge the gap between a student project and a career-defining asset.

steps.title: Define the MVP Core
steps.description: Identify the top three repetitive tasks in your stack (e.g., Auth, Stripe, Tailwind setup) and architect a modular starter kit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Lead Architect. Design a modular SaaS starter kit architecture using Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe. Focus on high-reusability components and clean dependency injection.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Initialize Next.js project with Supabase and Stripe integration'

steps.title: Implement Rapid Auth
steps.description: Don't reinvent the wheel. Use existing patterns to secure your starter kit, making it instantly deployable for users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure, production-ready authentication flow using Supabase Auth that includes magic links and social login providers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add-feature 'Supabase Auth with middleware protection'

steps.title: Bake in Developer Experience
steps.description: Marketing to devs is about DX. Add automated linting, pre-configured testing, and clear documentation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive documentation structure and setup script that automates environment variable configuration for new users.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Setup Husky, ESLint, and a README template for the starter kit'

steps.title: Build the Payment Rails
steps.description: Integrate Stripe to handle subscriptions, ensuring your starter kit is a revenue-ready foundation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a clean Stripe webhook handler that manages subscription status and user access levels in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add-feature 'Stripe Checkout and Webhook implementation'

steps.title: Deploy & Distribute
steps.description: Package your project for Vercel/Netlify so a user can clone and deploy in under 60 seconds.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Optimize the project build pipeline for Vercel and provide a 'Deploy to Vercel' button configuration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Optimize build scripts and finalize deployment configuration'

pro_tips: Treat your README as a landing page; use clear value propositions instead of just technical specs.
Build 'in public' by documenting your struggles with the starter kit on X or LinkedIn; this is your marketing.
Focus on one specific niche (e.g., 'SaaS for AI Agents') rather than a generic starter kit to reduce competition.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your boilerplate; it proves the code is production-grade.
Include a 'Starter Kit Roadmap' in your repo to show users exactly what features you plan to add next.
content.next_steps: After completing your starter kit, publish it to GitHub, share it in three relevant Discord communities, and write a blog post about one specific technical challenge you solved during the build process.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect moment to market your skills—create the tool that makes you indispensable. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
